description_tesim: Performer : "Kolo" Serbian State Folk Ensemble; Monire Vakili; Hossein Dehlavi; Orchestra of Fine Arts Administration of Iran; Ali Golab
Title from tape: Iranian & Serbian Recordings

Side I - Serbija; Igre 3 Servije (Dances from Serbia): Poskok; Durdevka; Igrale Se Delije; Dances from Pirot (E. Serbia); Sopsko Oro and Vlainica; Kacerac (with frula); Caj. Goro Carna (Hail Black Mountain); Ojimoja Ruzo Rumena (Oh, My Red Rose); Kraj Vardara; Gajde (dance); (Jugoton - LPM6 - (Zagreb, Ilica 213)). Yugoslav Folk Songs and Dances: Serbian Fo; Side II - Iran; 3 arranged folk songs: Reihane (Kurdish); Karwane (Kurdish); Shir Ali Mardan (Dezful); Katuli - Mazanderan (E. Caspian Province, accompanied by ney); Shor Shor (Kurdestan, W. Iran); Amina Way (Kurdestan, W. Iran); Giyan Giyan (Kurdestan, W. Iran); Bridal Song (Dezful, S. W. Iran); 45 rpm recordings Leyla Jan (Sistan, S. E. Iran); Sardar Amad Be Sistan (Sistan, S. E. Iran); Kharom Sharsharia; Garan Chapi Sorna - Dohol; Bivasa Donya

description_tesim: Side I An old miner named Sam - Delta Material in Dear Old Georgia. Jugo-Slavian Peasant Songs and Dances: Hop Saj Sasa - Croatia; Oj Mladosti Naj Milise Doba - Croatia; Sano Duso (sub-title: Vranjanka) - Serbia (Folkdancer records MH 3020; 78,B); Cacak - Serbia (Folkdancer Records MH 3022; 78,B); Krici Krici Ticek - Croatia; Poskakusa - Croatia; Kolo Kalendara - Croatia; Dance from Pirot - Servia (Sto Mi Omilelo); Makedonka - Serbia (Balkan 547; 78,A); Cujes Mala - Serbia; Kokonjeste - Serbia; Dajchovoto - Serbia; Serbian Medley: Poskok, Ti Momo, Djurdjevka, Igrale Se Delije. Side II Strike Music of American Labor: Hallelujah I'm a Bum (Greenway?); Pie in the Sky; Solidarity Forever; Union Maid - (a dub of the Almanac Singers record); Talking Union - (a dub of the Almanac Singers record); Sister Jenny's Turn to Throw the Ball; Ludlow Massacre - Woody Guthrie; Which Side - Almanacs; Join the NMU; Harry Sims; Fight for Union Recognition (tune: I've been workig on the railroad); The Boatmakers' Union is a No Good Union. Jane Fitzpatrick: An Ould Mon Came A-Courtin'; Oh Dear, What Shall Ee Dae; The Drowned Lover; Unnamed Tune.
Title from tape: Allen * Weide * Fitzpatrick
Performers: Jane Fitzpatrick, Sam (miner, last name unknown).

description_tesim: Origination : Evelyn Sitzer; Zena Schaffer; Myrna Novinsky
Performers: Fanny Sanchez; Jose Piedra; Carmelita Head; Avelina Castillo; Louise Demont

Content: Spanish Folklore; Lore of Holland. Side I: Comparisons and Proverbs; Comparisons and Proverbs. Side II: 10 Songs by Avelina Castillo [end Spanish Folk Speech]; Lore of Holland: Nichlas - song and customs associated with it; The Fifth of December - song; Santa Claus - song; Rhyme in Dutch - similar to Humpty Dumpty; Rhyme - about a little girl who went to the pawn shop and pawned her coat and then went to buy a lot of things; Discussion About People who Wear Wooden Shoes and Why; Song- about a mother's wish that her son will grow up to be a Captain; Pithain - song about the boy who brought in the Silver Fleet; Lullaby; Story about Hans and Fitche (Hansel and Gretal); National Holidays; Hide and Seek - game; Bim Bom Beya - counting out rhyme; Vie Vie Vye Vecht - counting out rhyme Green Pea Soup - how it is made; Ginger bread - how it is made; Meat Loaf - how it is made; Unbaked Apple Pie - how it is made; Frying Fish; The National Anthem of Holland
Title from tape: Spanish Folk Speech * Louise Demont

The mission of the UCLA Ethnomusicology Archive is to make its resources available to the faculty, staff, and students of the university community and to the general public. Title from tape: Yiddish Folk Speech #4 - Bass * Bonyben

Content: Various subjects. Locations: North Hollywood, California; Los Angeles, California; Ohai California; Canoga Park, California. Side 1A: Curse: I hope you go to your levia tomorrow; Curse (Yiddish): Afta Gonnuf Bren Ta Hittel; Expression (Yiddish): Reply to what's new: On the Roof...; General discussion; Jokes: Jew and Irishman in hospital; Jewish couple in court to obtain divorce; Variant of above; Jewish man writes to wife in old country and snds her things; Bald man meets man with hair; Couples spending their 25th wedding anniversary; A couple's experience with a Paris artist; Englishman marries; General discussion; Joke: Rabbi and Rabbitzen at the Mikveh. Side 1B: Bonybean History; Norht African Hunters and Fisherman; Meanterthal (The Tall Meander); Cro-Magnon (Grandpa-Cro); How the informant got the name "Burr"; How his family got to Central America (The Bonybean Tribe); Uncle Contiki; Origin of the saying "He's gone south"; Piedra Blanca; The invention of the bow; Discovering the ships of the sea; How lion canyon got its name; Little Shehan and Grandma Skinnyfingers; Grandma Skinnifingers' club; How the informant got the name "Wallace"; THe last of the Bonybean Tribe; How Wallace got a wife. Side 2A: Lil (poem); Here's to Mag (poem); Frigging in the rigging (good ship venus) (song); One-Eyed Reilly (song); Pilot Told Me Before He Died (song); Way Down by the River Perdec (song); Queen of all the Acrobats (song); Last Saturday Night (song); Good Old Mountain Dew (song); Meet Me by the Slop Shute (song); Story of a Cat - Sam the Fraternity Man (song); Gay Cavallero (song). Side 2B: West Point march; Presentation Day; The Fouth Class System; "Kissing Rock" on "Flirtation Walk"; The Stone Eagle on the doorway of Washington Hall; The Statue of General Sedgewick; The cadet of France statue from Le Col Polytechnique; West Point Alma Matre (origin and text of the song); The Core (origin and text of the song); Benny Haven's Oh (origin and text of the song); Army Blue (origin and text of the song); On Brave Old Army Team (song). Side 2C: The rock jumped when the rooster crowed (riddle); Trying to get bread, jam, and butter, to comeout even (riddle); The cows' legs matched the mountain (riddle); Come O Cao (tongue twister); Had an Old Mule (poem); Eeny-Meeny-Miney-Mo (counting out rhyme); Oh! The Buzzards, They Fly High (song). Tape #4, continued from tape 65/26. Note: Performers: Jeanette Machat; Louis Machat; Mike Hendin; Wallace Burr; Delta Songs; Christopher Lambides; Kathy Morgan
Locations: North Hollywood, California; Los Angeles, California.

description_tesim: Yiddish Expression used after curses if you want to make them ineffective;Yiddish Curse: Haben A Schlock;Yiddish Curse: Gae En Drerd;Yiddish Curse:  Mis A Mishena;Discussion of how various words are pronounced, etc.;Yiddish Curse:  Gae Cocken Af De Am and variant;Yiddish expression:  Don't Funfen Me;Discussion of meaning of Yiddish word "trend"Expression:  You Don't know ... from "Hinten" to a hole in the ground;Expression:  You Don't know from Borscht;Discussion about stories and songs about Rabbi and Rabbitzen;Discussion about the Shamas;Joke:  Shamas catches woman stealing talis;Discussion about various members (official) of Jewish community - moile, chasan, mulamed;Custom:  On Purim (Informant later corrected himself and sais this was done on Yom Kippur) A chicken is sacrificed;Custom:  Donation of charity and the #18;Expression:  Mema Caporra;Discussion about loaning money, then about Gentiles;Expression:  Pu, Pu, Pus A Goy's Cop;Believe:  Barial next to the fence;Custom:  Tearing Chricken;Expression:  Just like a goyisha cop;Interviewer's story about Jew who converted;Discussion about families with the name Cain (Cohen) and their position in Jewish Community;Belief:  Not looking in mirror when menstruating;Custom:  Slapping a girl when she first menstruates;Custom:  Sending a girl to Mikveh before she marries;Description of ceremony performed at sisters wedding;More discussion about family of Cohen;Discussion of Bar Mitzvah and participating in Minyan;Relation of dreams.
Location: North Hollywood, California; Los Angeles, California.

description_tesim: Title from tape: Matcha * Sephardic Lore
Performer: Mrs. Matcha, born in Turkey, 55 years old

Content: Sephardic Ballads. Side 1: War Song - 38 years ago - made up from letters written home; Moranica a Mi Mayaman (They Call Me the Dark One) - more than 200 years old; Love Song - More than 200 yeras old, tells of a young lover who's love was futile and frustrated and who died of tuberculosis; Song - 300 years old, about a Sultan who killed a young man; Background information; Song about a young girl who wanted to get married; Another song about a young girl who wanted to get married; Song about a young girl who is jealous of her mother and father's relationship and wants to get married; (another song not listed in notes). Side 2: Test tones, Indian music (not listed in notes).

Title from tape: Ollie Gilbert * Wilgus

Rose of White;Green Corn - banjo solo (first time she played the gourd banjo);Green Corn - Uncle Dave Macon Style - banjo solo;Little Brown Jog - banjo solo;Old Fashioned Sugar Hill - banjo solo;Soldier's Joy (she had sung bawdy verses previously for Ebby Wilgus, but would not record them.) - banjo solo;Black-eyed Susie - banjo solo;Waltz - title unknown - banjo solo;Waltz - title unknown - banjo solo;Fox Chase - banjo solo;Untitled banjo solo - (Goin' Downtown?);Lord Bateman (fragment);Come all you Brisk Young Cowboys;Song Composed by Joseph Loveny (?) on this death bed;(a discussion of songs she knows - with fragments - not listed on sheets);The Tom Cat Song (learned from her husband);Rattlesnakin' Daddy;(many fragments occur here);"On a dark stormy night the angels called mother" - (fragment) words Ollie read "somewhere" which she set to tune of "Precious Jewel";Haunted House;The Rich Lady (The Brown Girl);Old Joe Clark - (Fragment) - plus fragments "The Warship had landed" Nightingale etc.

repository_tesim: University of California, Los Angeles. Ethnomusicology Archive
dlcs_collection_name_ssm: Archive of California and Western Folklore
description_tesim: Title from tape: Yiddish Folk Speech #4 - Bass * Bonyben

Content: Various subjects. Locations: North Hollywood, California; Los Angeles, California; Ohai California; Canoga Park, California. Side 1A: Curse: I hope you go to your levia tomorrow; Curse (Yiddish): Afta Gonnuf Bren Ta Hittel; Expression (Yiddish): Reply to what's new: On the Roof...; General discussion; Jokes: Jew and Irishman in hospital; Jewish couple in court to obtain divorce; Variant of above; Jewish man writes to wife in old country and snds her things; Bald man meets man with hair; Couples spending their 25th wedding anniversary; A couple's experience with a Paris artist; Englishman marries; General discussion; Joke: Rabbi and Rabbitzen at the Mikveh. Side 1B: Bonybean History; Norht African Hunters and Fisherman; Meanterthal (The Tall Meander); Cro-Magnon (Grandpa-Cro); How the informant got the name "Burr"; How his family got to Central America (The Bonybean Tribe); Uncle Contiki; Origin of the saying "He's gone south"; Piedra Blanca; The invention of the bow; Discovering the ships of the sea; How lion canyon got its name; Little Shehan and Grandma Skinnyfingers; Grandma Skinnifingers' club; How the informant got the name "Wallace"; THe last of the Bonybean Tribe; How Wallace got a wife. Side 2A: Lil (poem); Here's to Mag (poem); Frigging in the rigging (good ship venus) (song); One-Eyed Reilly (song); Pilot Told Me Before He Died (song); Way Down by the River Perdec (song); Queen of all the Acrobats (song); Last Saturday Night (song); Good Old Mountain Dew (song); Meet Me by the Slop Shute (song); Story of a Cat - Sam the Fraternity Man (song); Gay Cavallero (song). Side 2B: West Point march; Presentation Day; The Fouth Class System; "Kissing Rock" on "Flirtation Walk"; The Stone Eagle on the doorway of Washington Hall; The Statue of General Sedgewick; The cadet of France statue from Le Col Polytechnique; West Point Alma Matre (origin and text of the song); The Core (origin and text of the song); Benny Haven's Oh (origin and text of the song); Army Blue (origin and text of the song); On Brave Old Army Team (song). Side 2C: The rock jumped when the rooster crowed (riddle); Trying to get bread, jam, and butter, to comeout even (riddle); The cows' legs matched the mountain (riddle); Come O Cao (tongue twister); Had an Old Mule (poem); Eeny-Meeny-Miney-Mo (counting out rhyme); Oh! The Buzzards, They Fly High (song). Tape #4, continued from tape 65/26. Note: Performers: Jeanette Machat; Louis Machat; Mike Hendin; Wallace Burr; Delta Songs; Christopher Lambides; Kathy Morgan

description_tesim: Recorded by Colin D. Edwards at UCLA for CBC Studios in Toronto

Side II Religious objects used to combat witches Churches' attitude toward witchcraft Court of law and witchcraft cases Geographical aspects of witchcraft beliefs Persecution of witches - down to today Stake-through-the-heart method of killing - in Northern Europe and America Discussion of Dr. Hand's Book Current sources of information on witchcraft *current beliefs Geometric designs to keep off witches Birds associated with witches Witchcraft due to need for scapegoat, sadism, mass hysteria Need for the Supernatural and fantastic, cultism Lack of witch-organization; witches' sabbath Flying Weather for witches Esoteric nature of witch-society. Lack of hangers-on in the normal world Scapegoat aspect: witch is the result of need for a scapegoat, people with idiosyncracies were singled out as a witch, witch beliefs always result from some real or fancied wrong
